q: How many players from one team are on a football pitch?
exp: Ten outfield players plus the goalkeeper.

q: Which body part may NOT touch the ball in football (except the goalkeeper)?
exp: Handling the ball is a foul — except for the goalkeeper in their area.

q: How many rings are on the Olympic symbol?
exp: The five rings represent the five inhabited continents.

q: Which sport uses a racket and a net to hit a small yellow ball?
exp: Tennis can be played on clay, grass or hard courts.

q: How many points is a free throw usually worth in basketball?
exp: Free throws are 1 point; normal baskets are 2 or 3.

q: How often are the Summer Olympic Games held?
exp: Summer and Winter Games alternate every 2 years.

q: In which sport does a 'KO' (knockout) happen?
exp: A KO is when a boxer can't get up before the count of 10.

q: Which of these is a water sport?
exp: Strokes include freestyle, backstroke, breaststroke and butterfly.

q: How many players from one team are on a volleyball court?
exp: In volleyball the ball must not touch your own side's floor.

q: Which trophy does the football World Cup winner receive?
exp: The football World Cup is held every 4 years.

q: In which sport do you score a 'home run'?
exp: A home run lets the batter run all the bases and score.

q: What is the aim in golf?
a: Get the ball in the hole in the fewest strokes
opts: Get the ball in the hole in the fewest strokes
exp: A golf course usually has 18 holes.
